mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding manufacturing and economic activity, leading to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in > 40% of months when INDPRO increases sequentially, over a 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below key thresholds indicates improving labor market conditions, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which supports equity prices over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative or flat in > 60% of instances when UNRATE crosses below 9.5% over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: With the federal funds rate at the zero lower bound, increases in payroll employment indicate that accommodative monetary policy is effectively stimulating the labor market, boosting equities as the recovery gains traction.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the combined FEDFUNDS-PAYEMS signal is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation environment (CPI below 220), moderate increases in consumer prices reflect growing demand without overheating, which is positive for corporate profits and equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in > 50% of quarters with CPI increases while CPI < 220.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in housing starts above 700,000 units signals renewed construction and housing demand, a leading indicator for economic growth that boosts short-term equity returns as investors anticipate broader recovery.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in > 55% of instances within 2 weeks of HOUST crossing above 700.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP reflects broad economic expansion, increasing corporate earnings and supporting higher stock market valuations over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns following positive GDP growth quarters are lower than median returns in all quarters over a 5-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and industrial production indicate synchronized demand and supply expansion, strengthening the economic recovery and driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When RSAFS and INDPRO both rise, S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 30% of instances over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment regime (UNRATE above 8%), increases in payroll employment are especially potent for equity returns because they signal the beginning of a durable labor market recovery, which historically boosts consumer confidence and spending.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in > 40% of months when PAYEMS increases while UNRATE > 8%.
